reasoning: The hypothesis `p.Prime ∧ p > 2` exactly encodes "odd prime". Expanding $f = (X+2)^p = \sum_{n=0}^{p} \binom{p}{n} 2^{p-n} X^n$, all coefficients are positive, so `f.support \ {0} = {1, ..., p}`. The sum $\sum_{n=1}^{p} \binom{p}{n} 2^{p-n} X^{n-1}$ is exactly $\frac{(X+2)^p - 2^p}{X}$, since $(X+2)^p - 2^p = \sum_{n=1}^{p} \binom{p}{n} 2^{p-n} X^n$ and dividing each term by $X$ gives the claimed sum. The coercion `(f.coeff n : Polynomial ℤ)` embeds the integer coefficient as a constant polynomial, which is the correct encoding. `Irreducible` on `Polynomial ℤ` is the standard notion of irreducibility in $\mathbb{Z}[x]$. The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
